clues.title: The Threat Letter
clues.description: A letter delivered to Sable's warehouse, written in Court of Smoke cipher. It demands the return of 'the Ember' within three days, or Sable dies. The cipher is Court standard, but the handwriting belongs to someone high in the organisation.

clues.title: The Ember
clues.description: A small, warm stone that glows with an inner fire visible only in darkness. Sable stole it from a Court vault because she recognised what it was: a Veil fragment, like the ones trafficked through the Gasworks, but older and more powerful. It does not whisper — it sings. And the song is beautiful, which makes it far more dangerous.

clues.title: Court Vault Records
clues.description: Records from the Court of Smoke's vault inventory, obtained through Sable's contacts. The Ember was catalogued as 'Item 47 — Origin: Gasworks recovery, Class: Extreme hazard, Status: Contained.' Someone in the Court's leadership authorised its storage rather than its destruction. The question is why.

clues.title: The Rival Fence
clues.description: A rival Court of Smoke fence named Greer has been publicly critical of Sable's operations. He has motive and opportunity. But Greer's threat is commercial, not lethal — he wants Sable's territory, not her life.
